top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Philippines' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
At mile 60 of 84, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
GFNY Philippines' mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Philippines. Train it until it feels boring.
GFNY Philippines' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 84 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,000ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 84 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 84 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At GFNY Philippines, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Tempo is GFNY Philippines' race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 84 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Philippines’s 84 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Philippines. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 84 miles of mixed terrain in Subic Bay, Philippines.
